title: Normal (worse) aging
summary: Normal (worse) aging.
detail: This is the less beneficial version of the SIRT1 longevity gene, which doesn't extend lifespan. It is normal in non-Asians. It has more cognitive decline with age. Taking resveratrol is also believed to increase longevity via the SIRT1 gene, so it might (or might not) be possible to mitigate this with resveratrol supplements or some other SIRT1 activator.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
conditions: This is the less beneficial version of the SIRT1 longevity gene, which doesn't extend lifespan. It is normal in non-Asians. It has more cognitive decline with age. Taking resveratrol is also believed to increase longevity via the SIRT1 gene, so it might (or might not) be possible to mitigate this with resveratrol supplements or some other SIRT1 activator.
release: SNPedia cached page export; page timestamp 2013-12-14T13:19:37Z
notes: SNPedia is supplementary and should not be treated as a primary clinical source.

title: Less mental decline with age
summary: Less mental decline with age.
detail: One copy of the good version of the SIRT1 longevity gene. This is believed to slow the aging of the brain, and perhaps to increase longevity.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
conditions: One copy of the good version of the SIRT1 longevity gene. This is believed to slow the aging of the brain, and perhaps to increase longevity.

title: Increased longevity. Less mental decline with age
summary: Increased longevity. Less mental decline with age.
detail: This is the best version of the SIRT1 longevity gene. This may be one of the genes that causes Asians to live longer than other races, as it is common in Asians and rare elsewhere. It is also the target of anti-aging supplements like resveratrol.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
